Notice Of Motion For Order Allowing Substitute Service Of Order Re Debtors Appearance And Examination
Off Calendar Apr-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mohammad Walizadeh, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett LLP, Tel. 415.777.3200, appearing in plaintiff / moving party Fletcher International, Ltd.;
Alphonse Fletcher, Jr., defendant in pro per, appeared.

Both sides stipulate that the matter may be heard by Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, Plaintiff Fletcher International, Ltd.'s, Motion For Order Allowing Substitute Service Of Order Re Debtors Appearance And Examination is Granted as modified: "Litigants have the right to choose their abodes; they do not have the right to control who may sue or serve them by denying them physical access." (Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1393.) Here, the evidence indicates defendant/Judgment Debtor Alphonse Fletcher, Jr. has repeatedly denied physical access to plaintiffs/Judgment Creditors to prevent personal service upon him at his residence within the St. Regis Residences, a condominium building in San Francisco. Further, on many occasions when the process server has unsuccessfully attempted personal service, the building security guard has called defendant regarding the process server and/or accompanied the process server to the door of Mr. Fletcher's residence. Thus substituted service upon the building security guard is appropriate. (See, e.g., Id. at 1392-1394.). In response to the tentative ruling, plaintiff's counsel provided evidence that defendant Alphonse Fletcher is the Chief Executive Office of defendant/Judgment Debtor Fletcher Asset Management, Inc. and also the statutory authority under Civ. Proc. Code 416.10 allowing for service of a summons on a corporation by serving the corporation's cheif executive officer. At the hearing, Mr. Fletcher informed the Court that he agrees to email serviceas personal service of all litigation documents at: afletcher@fletcher.com. Mr. Fletcher also affirms he is the CEO of Fletcher Asset Inc. and will accept litigation documents accordingly. Plaintiffs agree that email service will be service on them. The Court therefore orders that plaintiffs may accomplish service upon defendants Alphonse Fletcher, Jr. and Fletcher Asset Management, Inc., via substitute service of the Order re Debtors' Appearance and Examination upon the security guard at the St. Regis Residences at 188 Minna Street, Apartment 34C, San Francisco, California, 94105, in compliance with CCP section 415.20. Order signed. (See order filed this date.)

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/RBU)
